notes: Official Monarch product capture for TreasuryBench V1.
response:
For May 1–31, 2026, your recurring charges shake out into two main groups, plus one “in-between” bill.

Bucket 1: Subscriptions / fitness / software (~$172)
You had three clear recurring services on your Chase Sapphire Preferred 8600:

ClassPass on May 16: $89
Adobe on May 14: $60
Netflix on May 13: $23

Together, that’s about $172 in true subscriptions/fitness/software for May. These are solid ones to review for ongoing value or possible downgrades/cancellations.

Bucket 2: Phone / internet / utilities (~$72)

AT&T Wireless on May 9: $72 (Phone)

That gives you about $72 in recurring phone/internet/utility-type bills for May 2026.

Mixed item: Xfinity ($89)

Xfinity on May 5: $89, currently categorized as Utilities

You can treat Xfinity either way:

If you count it as a utility, Bucket 2 becomes about $161
If you count it as a subscription, Bucket 1 becomes about $261

There were no Rent charges in these filtered categories for May 2026, so nothing to exclude or flag separately there.
